content.full_text:
CITY OF RANCHO MIRAGE

OFFICE OF
COUNCILWOMAN
Sybil Jaffy
District #4

FPPC
AUG 2 0 1992
r

August 18, 1992

California Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street    Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804


Dear Sir:

I wish to determine if I can obtain a letter of immunity concerning
my interest in real property (my condominium residence) which may
be a factor in my ability to participate in decisions pertaining to the
developement of the Civic Center Project in the City of Rancho Mirage.
It is my understanding that Section 83114 sub-division A covers this request.

We are a city of approximately 10,000 people. We are divided into five
councilmanic districts with approximately 2000 persons in each. I
represent District 4 which contains a redevelopment agency area in
which a new civic center for this city is to be developed.

I have asked our city manager to prepare the statistical information
which according to the documents California's Conflict of Interest Law
for Public Officials and Legislative Amendments to the Political Reform
Act of 1974 as Amended to January 1, 1992, would be needed to make a
determination such as I am requesting. If there is additional information
that you require, I will provide it as soon as possible.

I appreciate your consideration of my request, and look forward to your
response.


Sincerely,

Sybil Jaffy
Councilmember, 4th District

enclosure

69-825 HIGHWAY 111 / RANCHO MIRAGE, CA 92270-2898 / (619) 324-4511 FAX (619) 324-8830

I can see this appears to be a mostly blank page with some very faint text or markings at the top that are not clearly legible in this image. The page appears to be numbered "1" in the top right corner, and there may be some other markings or text, but they are too faint or unclear for me to transcribe accurately.

Given the rules provided, I should indicate:

[illegible]

CITY OF RANCHO MIRAGE

MEMORANDUM

TO:        Councilwoman Sybil Jaffy

FROM:      Patrick M. Pratt, City Manager

SUBJECT:   Request for Information

DATE:      August 17, 1992

Upon your request for information on Friday, August 14th, concerning the number of residential units, businesses and population counts in and around the Civic Center Project, the Community Development Department has generated the following information. Please be advised this information should be considered estimates and has been developed based on a review of aerial photo proof top counts, existing maps and Chamber of Commerce information.

The following statistics apply to an area within a 2500 foot radius of the Civic Center site.

1.     Housing units - 898.

2.     Businesses - 120.

3.     Population - 1778.     This is based on 1.98 persons per household which is the number used in the latest 1990 census.

I hope this information is useful.     If you need any additional information or clarification on this material, please feel free to contact either myself or Bob Brockman who actually generated the numbers.

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or extremely faded, with only very faint marks visible at the top right corner that are too unclear to transcribe with confidence. The rest of the page is white/blank space.

CITY OF RANCHO MIRAGE

MEMORANDUM

TO:      Staff

FROM:    Patrick M. Pratt, City Manager

DATE:    February 26, 1992

SUBJECT: Population and Dwelling Units -
         Information to be made available
         to the public

In order to avoid confusion and conflicting data, please use the following statistics when you are asked for information about the population and number of dwelling units in Rancho Mirage.
